content: Just as language learners use personal reflective journaling (the core educational sense of reflexion in the high school curriculum) to reinforce weak vocabulary usage and correct writing missteps, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be adapted to incorporate structured, spaced "learning journal" prompts that explicitly log previous generation errors instead of only contrasting inconsistent solution perspectives. This builds a persistent, growth-aligned error memory that mirrors how human learners improve through repeated, intentional reflexion on past mistakes.
